candidates.stmt: A popular author of historical crime novels, David lives in Bodicote and is the only candidate who lives in the ward. He is also a parish councillor and is dedicated to serving the needs of our communities, campaigning to protect their rural nature and to preserve the green spaces between them. David loves getting out into the countryside and can often be found hiking in the fields around us. He was previously a civil servant dealing with business and local government. Having lived in the ward for many years, he understands its needs first-hand and pledges to put our villages first.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Putting parishes first.
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres

Linda Newbery taught English at an Oxfordshire comprehensive school for many years. A published writer of fiction since 1988, she became a full-time author in 2000, writing for young readers and adults as well as tutoring and mentoring. She is a member of the Four Villages Sustainability Network and is an active campaigner on environmental and animal issues.

I am delighted to once again stand as Conservative candidate for Calthorpe and Easington in the Cherwell District Council Elections 2026.  I have lived in Banbury for 15 years, and have been an active member of the Keep the Horton General campaign, a volunteer community driver for Citizens Advice North Oxon & South Northants, and a volunteer vaccination steward at the Banbury Cross Health Centre.

I was elected to Cherwell District Council in May 2022; later that year, I also became a Trustee of Banbury Charities. As a CDC Member for Banbury Calthorpe and Easington, I have played a key role in ensuring the opening of the Country Park and Pavilion, removal of the unsightly Heras fencing, maintenance within the Country Park  and much-needed improvements to the roads and other infrastructure in the Longford Park area, something that I continue to do.  I have also secured £10k for IT equipment for Longford Park Primary School from Oxfordshire County Council as well as supporting other grants to the School from Banbury Charities.

In May 2024, I was elected to Banbury Town Council, representing Calthorpe South. I continue to make sure that residents’ views and needs are fully represented, and I remain committed to pushing for ongoing improvements in the Longford Park, Bankside, and Cherwell Heights areas.

As well as being a councillor and charity trustee, I am also a volunteer at the weekly Banbury Larder Job Club, helping local residents to find work.

As a resident of Bloxham Vale, I look to join the hardworking Conservative team in standing up for residents.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

**About Alex**
Alex cares about people and community. She is standing as a Green Party candidate to become a councillor in Calthorpe & Easington because she wants to be in a position to do more for the community. Alex lives and works in Banbury and also volunteers with local charities and wants to build on that sense of community if she is elected as councillor. Alex has worked in the hospitality and retail sectors, working her way up from shop floor to management. 
Alex came to live and work in Britain from Lithuania 15 years ago. She has made her home here and lived and worked in Banbury for the last five years. 
**Running for District Council**
Alex understands that change starts in your home and your own community. She has talked and listened to many people in the communities of Calthorpe & Easington to understand the issues that affect residents most. If elected to represent this ward on Cherwell District Council, Alex will do everything in her power to help fix the issues that affect residents daily lives. 
**The Green Party CAN WIN** here in Calthorpe and Easington. Votes for **Green Party candidates are NOT wasted votes.** 
The Green Party has gained a huge increase in support, both locally and Nationally over the last nine months. Membership has more than tripled nationally and quadrupled locally. The decisive Green win in the recent parliamentary by-election in Gorton and Denton and the decisive council by-election win in Margate where Greens overturned the incumbent Reform vote, serve to demonstrate what the Green Party can achieve.
Many people who have previously supported other parties are telling Alex they will be voting Green in this election. People are appreciating the Green Party message of hope and inclusion over the politics of division and exclusion espoused by other contenders and the failed policies of the Labour and Conservative two-party dominance.
If elected, Alex will prioritise the issues residents are telling her about:
**Top of the list

I am standing as a Green Party councillor in Banbury Cross and Neithrop because I am passionate about doing the best for our local area.

Having listened to many Banbury locals, I understand the following as key areas of concern: 
• terrible road surfaces and potholes, 
• keeping and improving the Horton Hospital, 
• empty shops on out Highstreet and in Castle Quay shopping centre 
• struggling with capacity in our schools for growing numbers of children 
• poorly planned roadworks
• a lack of activities, places and spaces for our young people.

All of these are key areas of concern for locals and in my capacity as a District Councillor, I promise to work hard with the necessary authorities to tackle these issues.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

I am standing as an independent candidate for the CDC ward of Cross & Neithrop. I believe that people are disillusioned with party politics and are eager for change. I will make decisions based upon facts and not party affiliation or the whims of a donor. 
I am from a scientific background and work as a chemist. I apply the same scientific methodology of using available evidence towards making decisions upon policy. 
I feel that to make Banbury a better place to live we need to look at the following areas. 
Poverty: the cost of living has increased for everyone, but it affects the poorest and most vulnerable the most. We should provide more equity so that everyone gets the tools and resources they need to succeed. 
Housing: properly affordable social housing is desperately required in Banbury. Young people can not afford to buy and will be forced to move away from the area. Rental prices are always increasing , and what social housing that there is; is not always maintained properly by housing associations. 
Jobs: We need to encourage more businesses to Banbury, especially ones that will provide high skilled and high waged jobs. This would also help stop the exodus of ambitious young people away from Banbury. These are the people who will spend money in the town and create a booming economy for all

Our ward contains some of the most vital infrastructure in Banbury, yet we never seem to be prioritised, a vote for me will help me change that.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

I am proud to be standing as the Liberal Democrat candidate for Banbury Grimsbury & Hightown. I want to offer residents a positive alternative to political division — focusing instead on practical, common-sense ideas that make the neighbourhood a better place to live and work.

Like many people across Banbury, I care deeply about the everyday issues that affect the community — from improving the look and feel of streets and public spaces to ensuring the right infrastructure and services are in place to support residents. I believe it is important that everyone has a real choice on the ballot, and that Liberal Democrat values of fairness, community and local decision-making are represented.

I support a more pragmatic, locally focused approach to politics — one that puts residents first and works constructively to get things done.

In a closely contested ward, where the outcome could go in different directions, every vote for the Liberal Democrats is a vote for practical, common-sense solutions — and against division and confrontation.

My name is Andrew Crichton, and I am your Labour County Council candidate in the Oxfordshire County Council elections on 1st May 2025.

Since I was first elected as your District Councillor in 2022, I have been trying to fix issues with our local infrastructure, adoptions of estates and advocate for residents with the County Council.

I have been left frustrated by a lack of engagement from current County Councillors for our area, and so I decided to stand to be your County Councillor on 1st May.

Hardwick residents should have a County Councillor who is proactive and contactable - but that simply isn't the case at the moment.

Please support me on 1st May and I will always be working on your side to try and improve things locally.

Simi Cumberbatch, a rooted, practical voice for Banbury, Hardwick

Simi Cumberbatch was born in Banbury and has spent her life rooted in the town she cares about. She lives locally, keeps an allotment, and deals with the same everyday issues as her neighbours, from potholes to stretched services and rising housing costs. When residents say worn roads make travel harder, that local infrastructure is under pressure, and that soaring house prices create uncertainty, Simi isn’t surprised. These are exactly the concerns people raise with her on the doorstep and the issues she is determined to tackle.

Simi brings a rare combination of environmental expertise, frontline healthcare experience and a hands‑on approach to community problems. Having worked in NHS pharmacy with GP surgeries and local hospitals, she understands the human stories behind the headlines: missed appointments, delayed treatments, and the stress that comes when services are at breaking point. In her current role in the environmental sector, she sees how everything connects such as planning, flooding, wildlife, transport and economic pressures and works to protect people, nature and local places. Her work includes reviewing environmental information, supporting flood‑resilience planning, safeguarding habitats and enabling sustainable, well‑planned growth.

Simi doesn’t just talk about community; she shows up for it. As a Banbury Flood Warden, she helps spot early warning signs and get problems dealt with before they grow. She volunteers on local litter picks and community improvement projects because she believes that consistent, practical action builds stronger, safer neighbourhoods. When sheds on her allotment site were burgled, Simi turned detective: she used tracking devices on her tools, helped the police recover stolen items, and showed calm under pressure, attention to detail and a willingness to stand up for her community.

Simi is standing to give Banbury Hardwick a stronger, better‑informed vo

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency

recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

Hello, I'm Michael Offei (you can also call me Mike), and I'm standing to be your Liberal Democrat councillor for Hardwick ward on May 7th.

I live in Hardwick with my family. I see the same problems you do every single day. I'm not a career politician - I'm your regular neighbour who's fed up with empty promises.

𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐈 𝐒𝐞𝐞
- Families moving into estates with limited or no public transport connection.
- Young people with nowhere to go and nothing to do, then blamed for anti-social behaviour.
- Elderly residents struggling to access services that should be there for them.
- Developers building houses with no shops, no facilities, no infrastructure.
- Councillors raising concerns but delivering nothing. Either because they don't have the power to act or because they're toeing political party lines.

𝐖𝐡𝐲 𝐈'𝐦 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠
I believe Hardwick (which includes Hardwick Hill, Hanwell Fields, Hardwick Estate, Southam Road corridor homes, farms and everywhere in between) has incredible potential. We're a diverse, vibrant community with families who care deeply about where we live.

I'm tired of sitting on the fence and watching us get left behind. We deserve councillors who can actually get things done, not just raise concerns from the sidelines. But potential means nothing without delivery. I believe;
*Housing should come with infrastructure, not just houses
*Young people deserve investment, not abandonment
*Our elderly and vulnerable deserve dignity and support
*Green spaces are precious and must be protected
*Small businesses create jobs and should be championed
*Councils should serve residents, listen and communicate openly.

𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐈𝐬𝐧'𝐭 𝐋𝐞𝐟𝐭 𝐯𝐬 𝐑𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭. 𝐈𝐭'𝐬 𝐀𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐆𝐞𝐭𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 𝐃𝐨𝐧𝐞, 𝐀𝐧𝐝 𝐃𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐑𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭 𝐖𝐚𝐲.

I'm not promising miracles. I'm promising hard work, transparency and genuine accountability.

If you're frustrated with empty promises - 𝐈 𝐚𝐦 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐭𝐞
If you want infrastructure before houses - 𝐈 𝐚𝐦 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐭𝐞
If you believe councils should listen to residents,

Bryonie has lived locally in Bicester all her life and works as a carer in the community, giving her an understanding of the challenges local people face. Bryonie wants to represent Bicester East with the passion and energy residents deserve.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

I have lived in Bicester Town Centre for just over 9 years. I have 1 son who attends Longfields school. I can often be found taking advantage of the various 5k healthy routes around Bicester with my family walking our 2 dogs.

My husband is a veteran and I am passionate about supporting our armed forces which is why I’m incredibly proud to have achieved free parking in council car parks on Remembrance Sunday from this year.

I have worked in finance since leaving school and over the past 28 years alongside studying for various professional qualifications progressed to Head of Finance for an international IT business.

I currently work as a Finance Manager for a Management Consulting Company having taken a step back for family and community.

I am passionate about the future of the area we all call home and have a reputation at work for being the go to person to get things done and who would know the answer or could find it if even out of my remit.

My areas of interest are:

* Improve Play Parks - including continuing to push for a Splash Park at Garth Park.

* Oppose any exploration into 3 weekly green bin collections.

* Stand with Firefighters against the planned cuts in Oxfordshire.

* Introduction of Banking Hubs in Libraries

* Improve Pioneer Square by holding those responsible to account.

* Reverse the plans to close the toilets at Pioneer Square.

* Ensuring EWR and OCC consider the impact of the London Road Crossing plans on the Launton Road and ensure mitigation is in place and not just ignored.

* Holding OCC to account for the state of our roads. (My camera role is mostly pictures of the potholes I’ve reported)

* Budgets/Spending getting the best value and efficient use of council funds.

I want to be here to help and support the community as a whole and want to be available to listen to all areas of concern. Over the last 5 years I’ve maintained a Facebook page full of information to keep the community informed.

Having lived in Bicester for eleven years I have watched it grow, develop and change. As a Green Party candidate I stand to support the changes we are facing to help both  people and the natural environment on which we all depend. I have particular interest in:

- the economic challenges faced by Bicester residents such as the need for  truly affordable housing
- the increasing pressure on our green spaces - which means we need to improve air and water quality, stop fly-tipping and promote real biodiversity 
- community-led decision making - to involve residents directly in shaping local plans and budgets

Vote for the Green Party to choose what matters.
Julius has spent his career delivering results from people who need help from the local council. This means he has the right skills and experience to make sure residents get the support they need.

As a resident of Bure Park, I am standing for election to represent our community and voice the concerns and issues to the District council.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

I’m a proud family man and a former member of the British Army, where I served for over 20 years. My time in the Army taught me the value of service, responsibility, and looking out for others — values I believe are vital for a strong local community.
I am standing for the Bicester North and Caversfield ward because I care deeply about the area and the people who live here. I believe in building a strong, supportive community, creating opportunities for our young people, and ensuring our neighbourhoods remain clean, safe, and welcoming for families. I want to listen to residents, work hard on their behalf, and help make our community an even better place to live.

Waste & Environment

Cleaner streets, better services

Reliable refuse collection in neglected areas
Fair, consistent council services
Cleaner neighbourhoods we can be proud of

Housing & Planning

Homes for those who serve and those in need

Fairer access to council housing for Veterans and Service families
Stronger support for vulnerable residents to prevent homelessness
Planning that puts local people first

Community & Leisure

Supporting young people, strengthening our community

More youth and community projects
Safe, positive spaces for young people
Working together to build community pride

Chris has been a resident of Bicester for almost 30 years, seeing a lot of changes in the area, with many being positive changes.

Working in retail he knows how hard things can be for those who are having to work extra hours, trying to save money for something and seeing others suffer while not being able to help. That’s why Chris believes that those that represent us should remember they have a responsibility to the public over anyone else.

“As a councillor I will listen to people and not just tell you what I think you want. This is why I became involved in local politics,” said Chris. “I can’t promise to solve every problem, but I do commit to doing my best, to listen and to keep in touch regularly.”

I’ve lived in Bicester South & Ambrosden for more than two decades, raising my family here and being part of this vibrant community.

I’ve spent my career running and managing major infrastructure projects, giving me the skills to help shape investment so our growing town has infrastructure that is fit for the future.

I believe in serving others with integrity, responsibility, and a strong sense of community. I want to see our high streets flourish, streets feel safe, and local voices shape our future as we work together to continue building a better Bicester for everyone.

I am Shams Ud-din, the Conservatives’ candidate standing up for Bicester South, Kingsmere, Langford, New Langford, Gravenhill and Ambrosden. I have lived in Kingsmere for more than 10 years and I am proud to be hands-on in our community—organising family fun days, educational activities, summer camps, youth programmes, neighbourhood food festivals cultural events, and supporting local welfare initiatives. Over the last few years, I’ve seen things take a turn for the worse and too many decisions that don’t reflect residents’ priorities. If we want change locally, we have to change who runs the council. As your councillor, I will be visible, hardworking and accountable because every decision I take will affect my family as much as yours.

Damien is a serving Town Councillor for the Bicester South Ward and was the Mayor of Bicester for 2024-25. He believes in promoting compassion and belonging – where each one of our neighbours feels welcome, regardless of race, age, gender or ability. He is committed to a world that works for everyone.

I am proud to stand as your Reform UK candidate for Bicester West.
This campaign is not about politics as usual — it’s about real people, real experiences, and real change.

I have spent the last 10 years building a successful local business here in Oxfordshire. Through that journey, I’ve worked with families, businesses, and organisations across our community. I understand the pressures people are facing every day — from rising costs to overstretched services — because I see it first-hand.
But my journey hasn’t been easy.

I was diagnosed with ADHD, and from a young age I faced challenges, particularly throughout my education. It wasn’t always understood, and that made things harder. Even now,

I continue to face the realities of ADHD in day-to-day life.
However, those challenges have shaped who I am today.

They have given me:
Resilience
Determination
A strong work ethic

The ability to think differently and solve problems
Alongside my campaign,

I am committed to raising awareness of ADHD — both in business and in the wider public. As an ambassador for ADHD,

I want to:

Help people better understand ADHD
Support businesses in creating more inclusive workplaces

Break down stigma around neurodiversity

Ensure people are not overlooked or misunderstood

I believe that everyone deserves to be heard, understood, and supported — regardless of their background or challenges.

My reasons for standing are simple:

To be a strong, local voice for Bicester West
To bring practical, real-world experience into decision-making
To stand up for our community and ensure it is not overlooked
To represent people who feel unheard

I am not a career politician. I am someone who has built a business from the ground up, faced challenges, and understands the realities of everyday life.

I will:
Listen to residents
Stand up for our community
Push for practical solutions
Work hard to deliver real results

My Priorities for Bicester West
Getting Development Right
Cleaner, Safer, Inclusive

When families in Bicester West have needed someone to fight their corner — Claire Brenner has been there.

**A Local Champion** 
For years, Claire Brenner has spoken up for vulnerable families and children, particularly those affected by the national SEND crisis, challenging decisions where systems have fallen short and pushing for the support families are entitled to.

Claire has worked across the political spectrum putting people before politics and focusing on what actually delivers change. This isn’t about party lines — it’s about doing what is right. She regularly appears on local news and BBC radio as a spokesperson for SEND families.

Alongside this, Claire supports families directly — helping them navigate complex services, attending meetings, and ensuring their voices are heard when they might otherwise be overlooked.

Claire became a SEND campaigner because she had to leave her career in cancer research to care for her disabled son after he was failed by his school. She vowed to do all she could to ensure no other family would go through what hers did.

**Keeping London Road Open**
Claire Brenner first championed the idea for an underpass back in 2023, and launched a campaign that resulted in  the underpass proposal being included in the official consultation. Calum Miller MP spoke of Claire’s campaign in the House of Commons.

Claire is delighted that all sides, including East West Rail now support plans for a shallow underpass for cars, cyclists, and pedestrians. She will continue working with anyone, regardless of party, to ensure it is delivered.

Claire believes this is exactly what local politics needs more of — genuine cross-party collaboration focused on delivering what residents actually need and deserve.

**The Greens can win in Bicester West**
Claire is endorsed by the Councillor who is stepping down after 8 years.
_“I know that Claire will make an excellent councillor and I wholeheartedly support her campaign to replace me in Bicester West.”_
*

Tim is a local resident, school governor and Secretary of a local Residents Association.

Professionally, he works in a senior specialist role managing global programmes with budgets in the tens of millions. His work focuses on ensuring money is spent wisely, policies are effective, and decisions are based on evidence and accountability.

He has worked in highly regulated environments and is experienced in governance, financial oversight and improving how complex systems operate.

Locally, Tim is actively involved in strengthening community structures and helping things run effectively behind the scenes.

**Tim’s focus:**
- Better use of public money and stronger accountability
- Transparent and fair decision-making
- Improving communication between council and residents
- Delivering practical, workable solutions

I’m standing as the common-sense candidate for Cropredy, the Sibfords and Wroxton. Having worked in the NHS since 2012 with the vast majority of my career being in the Ambulance Service in North Oxfordshire, I’m a practical problem solver with true understanding of the real-life problems individuals and communities face on a daily basis. 
In my experience; division does not deliver. Inter-agency working is at the heart of what I do and so I am dedicated to representing you as a true believer in teamwork with you as the priority.
With your support I will bring an empathetic ear and a voice that delivers as a local person with a real stake in our area.

My name is Anne Cullen and I am hoping to become your Labour and Cooperative Party Councillor for Calthorpe and Easington. I have lived in this area for the last 37 years. I am a registered social worker and I worked for Oxfordshire County Council Social Services serving children, young people and families in Banbury and the surrounding area. Later I worked in a large hospice providing care for dying people and their families. Nowadays I work part time supporting staff who work in the NHS, social care and hospices. I also work on voluntary basis to improve care for dying people who are homeless or living in poverty.  My professional career has been about service and I believe this experience has equipped me well to become an effective and committed councillor. Outside of work my husband and I love being busy grandparents and this is one of many reasons why I want to be part of building a better future for people of all ages in this area, including affordable housing and better transport, ending fuel poverty and protecting the environment.

We at the SDP are very committed to providing greater focus to Family, Community and Nation. In order that we identify our worth and can build stronger ties in our community and to be a proud citizen of the United Kingdom.

I am a resident of Cropredy and have been for 8 years. I have been a serving police officer for 33 years of my life, a college lecturer, worked at both the Ashmolean Museum and Bodleian Library. During Covid I worked as a carer, and up until recently in reception/security at Wroxton College.

1. I believe education is such an important part of life, but we seem to be failing young people in schools and colleges, with a lack of discipline, funding, support and in so many cases lack of providing options for school leavers (although the latter also includes society in general), and not forgetting the most recent disaster, stating that our local children are not able to access Banbury schools and have to attend a school in Bicester. ACCOUNTABILITY!

2. The SDP are seeking public ownership of both the rail network and local water authorities. The cost of fares rising every year and lack of facilities on the trains is way out of control. The same of course can be said about the water authorities. The ultimate insult to injury, is the outrageous bonuses being paid to senior management, who have proven records of failing in their roles of providing safe waters to drink and swim in. ACCOUNTABILITY!

3. Affordable housing for many years was an issue for the towns & cities of our land, but now it is of great concern throughout the UK. New housing is of course a necessity, but to suggest it’s affordable is an insult to our intelligence. ACCOUNTABILITY!

4. Crime and Policing. In our rural communities we are constantly being made aware of incident's involving suspicious activity and people, via social media! We have definitely been left to fend for ourselves as a result of horrendous cut backs to policing (this like many other issues is another concern throug

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Putting parishes first.
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

I have lived in Deddington ward all my life. If elected, I would plan to fight to swing the balance of power away from large developers and towards council housing and smaller developers who build in consultation with existing residents.

I would also fight for new developments to be carbon neutral and fully insulated where achievable, and use all tools at the council’s disposal to deal with the climate emergency on a local level.

I believe Fringford and The Heyfords need a representative who understands how the Council works but isn't afraid to challenge its direction when it stops serving rural residents.

I am not a career politician; I am a former councillor and local resident who refuses to let our villages be overlooked in favour of the larger towns.

As a former Cabinet Portfolio Holder, I know that local government is most effective when it focuses on the basics: clean streets, fair planning, and financial discipline. Here is how I plan to apply that experience to our ward:

Protecting Our Rural Character: I am a firm believer that infrastructure must come before expansion. We’ve seen too many "unnecessary" developments that threaten our green spaces and put a strain on our local roads, water system and schools. I will demand transparent planning processes that give our communities a real seat at the table.
Fixing Waste Management:  I am deeply concerned by the potential move toward three-weekly bin collections alongside the current requirement to book appointments at the local tip. This is a recipe for increased fly-tipping in our countryside. I will push for a "common sense" approach to waste—making it easier, not harder, for you to dispose of rubbish responsibly.

Championing Our Farmers: Our rural economy is built on agriculture. I want to refocus the Council on supporting local farmers and sustainable agriculture, ensuring that Cherwell’s policies protect—not penalise—those who maintain our landscape.
Fiscal Responsibility: Every penny of your Council Tax must be justified. My past and focus is on ensuring efficient operations and transparent financial management to save residents money across the district.

I have a proven track record of tackling the "unseen" but vital aspects of governance—rigorous financial management and collaborative decision-making. I am ready to put that experience to work immediately to save you money and protect the unique rural character of Fringford,

It seems like nothing ever changes whichever government gets into power. Same dire priorities: cutting taxes for the rich, cutting benefits and services to the rest of us, putting a quick profit before long term health, security and happiness. No wonder voter turnout is so low!

The Green Party offers a real alternative. We believe passionately in social justice, a fairer distribution of wealth, better services for all and an end to the headlong rush towards climate disaster.

Vote Green! The party for real change.

Fiona has been a Cherwell District Councillor for 4 years, a Parish councillor for 11 years in Yarnton and 7 years in Kidlington (now a Town Councillor). Her focus is to engage with residents to create community projects that connect people with our environment.

She is currently the Deputy Chair of Yarnton Parish Council and sits on the Community Committee at Kidlington Parish Council where she and is also on the allotment committee. She was instrumental in setting up a community orchard in Yarnton and is working towards similar projects in Kidlington, including shared garden spaces in public parks that will help people to grow their own food. She's also a strong supporter of animal rights and a campaigner for wildlife protection.

She has been involved with local campaign groups and parish councils in monitoring the huge development plans for the area, making sure that developers are addressing flooding issues, ecological impacts and traffic management. She has been campaigning for more safe cycle routes and better public transport and is a founder member of the local flood resilience group as well as being an active member of the Kidlington Environment Group.

Fiona has been a volunteer at the local community larder and also helped with the washing up and table settings for the local Climatarian kitchen lunch club. She also helped with food deliveries to vulnerable people during the early days of the pandemic. She was the lead instigator of low noise fireworks in the annual Kidlington display which makes the event more accessible to all people and less damaging to wildlife and pets. She also helped compile the 'Gardening from the ground up' booklet that was given away free to local allotment holders. She has also been a strong advocate for managing local green spaces sensitively, like the green roundabout at the junction between Mulcaster and Croft Avenues.

I’ve spent 16 years as a public servant in the Ministry of Defence, managing complex, high-value projects and ensuring every pound of taxpayers’ money is used wisely.

Now, I’m standing to deliver practical, local solutions — focusing on safer streets, reliable services, and real value for our community, while listening to and acting on your priorities.

Over the past weeks, I’ve been out across Kidlington East — knocking on doors, delivering leaflets, and walking the streets. Most importantly, I’ve been taking the time to listen.

And one message keeps coming back: people feel unheard. Brushed off. Left behind.

That isn’t right. And it’s time for change.

If elected, I will make sure your voice is heard — and that Kidlington East gets the attention it deserves.

Gabriel lives in Kidlington and is passionate about helping local people get the support they need. Gabriel has the knowledge and skills to deliver results for residents and wants to put these to use as a local councillor.

I am campaigning for:
✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres

I'm Ian Hodgson, Chairperson for Reform UK Bicester & Woodstock and a father committed to community and opportunity. With over 20 years leading in manufacturing industries – rising from engineer to Operations Director – I know firsthand the importance of efficiency, managing budgets, and developing skills. Government should operate with the same common sense, yet we see waste and bureaucracy hindering progress.

I'm deeply concerned by the skills gap limiting our young people and the visible decline in our NHS, which I've experienced personally. Skilled staff are leaving due to poor conditions. Reform UK offers practical solutions: cutting waste, investing smartly in training and apprenticeships, and vital measures like income tax cuts for frontline health staff. It’s time to ditch slow-moving politics and apply a decisive, business-minded approach to get things done, delivering value and real opportunities. I’m committed to driving that change.
